arts.ttl: Barcelona considering ingenious way to lower asking price of Viktor Gyokeres
arts.sum: Barcelona are looking to the striker market ahead of the 2025 summer transfer window, as they aim to secure the services of Robert Lewandowski’s successor. In recent months, the name of Viktor Gyokeres has featured prominently amongst the speculation, with the Swedish striker having already amassed 33 goals for club and country this season. Gyokeres […] The post Barcelona considering ingenious way to lower asking price of Viktor Gyokeres appeared first on Football España.

arts.sum: A debate has been kickstarted behind the scenes at La Liga giants Barcelona, over the potential signing of Liverpool star Mohamed Salah. That’s according to Mundo Deportivo, who have on…

arts.ttl: Why isn’t Lamine Yamal playing for Barcelona against Brest? Hansi Flick’s ‘setback’ injury update
arts.sum: Of the 15 games in which Yamal started this season, Barcelona have won 14. Barcelona will be without teenage sensation Lamine Yamal as they host Brest in the Champions League on Tuesday.

arts.ttl: Romano confirms Barcelona 'on track' for mega double deal
arts.sum: Barcelona are closing in on new contracts for Pedri and Gavi, according to Fabrizio Romano. Gavi, meanwhile, made his return from a year-long absence due to an ACL injury recently and started his first game against Celta Vigo on Saturday in what ultimately proved not a particularly happy occasion as Barcelona surrendered a comfortable two-goal lead.

arts.ttl: Where could Mo Salah go next if he leaves Liverpool? Barcelona love a free transfer, the Saudi Pro League could offer the star record-breaking payday - or Premier League rivals might try to lure him away from Anfield
arts.sum: Mohamed Salah could find himself in a situation come May whereby he has two hands on the Premier League title and one foot already out of the exit door at Liverpool amid a current contract standoff.

arts.ttl: Brest lineup and tactics accidentally leaked ahead of Barcelona clash
arts.sum: Barcelona may have been given an unexpected leg up ahead of Tuesday night's Champions League clash with Brest at the Estadi Olimpic Lluis Companys after a whiteboard seemingly showing the French side's tactics was seen during training on Monday evening.
